title: Decimal and Octal Character Values
text: Decimal and Octal Character Values Decimal or octal values allow you to define special characters. Decimal Values: ^ nnn Octal Values: ^/ nnn Arguments nnn —up to three digits: 000 through 255 for decimal values 000 through 377 for octal values Description If a mnemonic for a character is not available, or if you prefer to do it this way, you can enter the decimal or octal value for each character. It is not possible to enter anything other than Uniface Font 0 characters in a translation table, so using decimal or octal values allows you to define special characters. Specific definitions of special characters should always use this syntax. For example, both ^66 and ^/102 are interpreted as the ASCII character B . Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
